workflow_runs.display_title: feat: v0.3.0 — vertical zoom, suggestion labels, label positioning fixes

head_commit.message:
feat: v0.3.0 — vertical zoom, suggestion labels, label positioning fixes

- Vertical frequency zoom: Shift+Wheel, V-slider, freq scrollbar, freq axis drag
- Suggestion labels: BirdNET detections with construction-fence border, accept/discard
- XC loading UX: no-key modal, topbar indicator, auto re-import, URL sync
- Right sidebar: settings panel, properties panel (pin/hover, inline editing)
- Label sidebar: hierarchical origin→species grouping, sticky headers
- Annotation toolbar: draw-mode toggle, copy/paste
- Language selector moved to topbar
- Toolbar wraps to two rows instead of More button

Fixes:
- Label positions on window resize / monitor change (spectrogram redraw in handler)
- Label horizontal jitter on zoom slider (deferred zoomchange event)
- Middle-mouse vertical drag inversion
- Zoom capped at 450 px/s

Version bumped to 0.3.0 (VERSION, package.json, pyproject.toml, storybook badge).
README updated with new features and labeling app demo link.
CHANGELOG updated with full v0.3.0 entry.

workflow_runs.name: CI
workflow_runs.display_title: fix: use union-find clusters for consistent swimlane depth

head_commit.message:
fix: use union-find clusters for consistent swimlane depth

The per-region local depth approach could produce visual overlaps when
a region straddled two areas of different depth (e.g. a region from a
3-deep area extending into a 2-deep area: it kept 33% height while its
neighbor got 50% height, causing them to overlap visually).

Now overlapping regions are grouped into clusters via union-find.
Within each cluster, all regions share a uniform depth (= number of
distinct rows used) and dense local-row indices, so top/height never
conflict. Isolated regions or separate clusters keep independent sizing.

Sorted-start early-break optimisation keeps the O(n²) overlap check
fast for the typical case of mostly non-overlapping annotations.

workflow_runs.display_title: fix: local swimlane stacking for waveform annotation regions

head_commit.message:
fix: local swimlane stacking for waveform annotation regions

Previously, when annotations overlapped, all regions were divided into
globally uniform rows (e.g. 3 overlapping → every region got 33% height).
Now the row stacking is computed locally: each region measures the depth
(number of occupied rows) only within its own time span.

Non-overlapping regions keep full height; only genuinely stacked groups
shrink. A dense local-row index ensures correct vertical positioning
relative to the active overlap group, not the global row count.

workflow_runs.display_title: fix: redraw spectrogram when overview label track rows change

head_commit.message:
fix: redraw spectrogram when overview label track rows change

When labels are loaded or added, .overview-label-tracks grows by 14px
per unique species row, shrinking .spectrogram-container (flexbox).
The canvas height and CoordinateSystem were stale, causing mouse-Y to
frequency mapping to drift — visible as a vertical offset when drawing
labels at lower frequencies.

Fix: _renderOverviewLabelTracks() now tracks row count changes and
triggers _queueResizeRedraw(), which defers to the next animation
frame (after DOM reflow), redraws the spectrogram canvas at the
correct height, updates coords, and emits viewresize so label overlay
layers re-render with current dimensions.

head_commit.message:
fix: resolve TypeScript errors blocking pre-push hook

- dsp.js: add colourScale to @returns JSDoc; add non-null JSDoc casts
  for denseFB, sparseFB, powerBuf (TS can't narrow through if/else
  branches with conditional null initialization)
- spectrolipiEngine.js: add computeProgressive async generator stub
  (single-shot yield) matching the interface from createSpectrogramProcessor
- Fixes 10 tsc --noEmit errors (8 TS18047, 1 TS2741, 1 TS2339)

workflow_runs.display_title: refactor: deduplicate clamp, mel conversion, maxBin, freq↔pixel

head_commit.message:
refactor: deduplicate clamp, mel conversion, maxBin, freq↔pixel

DRY improvements:
- Export clamp() and clampNumber() from utils.js; remove local copies
  from annotations.js and PlayerState.js
- Import hzToMel/melToHz from dsp.js in coordinateSystem.js instead
  of inline mel formulas
- Extract computeMaxBin() from coordinateSystem.js as shared export;
  reuse in buildSpectrogramGrayscale (spectrogram.js)
- Rewrite standalone pixelYToFrequency/frequencyToPixelY in
  spectrogram.js as thin wrappers around CoordinateSystem

head_commit.message:
feat(labeling-app): add origin, author, merge overlapping labels

- Add 'Origin' and 'Author' columns to the labels table
- BirdNET detections get origin='BirdNET', manually created labels get origin='manual'
- Add optional author input field in the BirdNET modal
- Add 'Merge overlapping labels' checkbox (default on) that merges
  same-species detections with overlapping time ranges
- Include confidence, origin, and author in JSON export
- Responsive: hide origin/author columns on small screens

head_commit.message:
feat(birdnet): bundle BirdNET v2.4 TF.js model for GH Pages, optional for npm

- Add models/birdnet-v2.4/ with TF.js layers model + labels (78 MB)
- Demo build (vite.demo.config) copies models/ into _site/ output
- Labeling app defaults model URL to bundled relative path
- Export BIRDNET_MODEL_URL constant (GH Pages CDN fallback)
- Add package.json export entry ./models/birdnet-v2.4
- npm package excludes models/ via files allowlist (opt-in only)
- Add .gitattributes to mark .bin shards as binary
